# agentconnect

> Write your MCP server + hooks (and commands / skills / subagents) ONCE; install them across every detected AI-agent CLI in its native config dialect — and get default, platform-independent, local-first per-tool token telemetry.

## What it is

Middleware that solves two recurring problems for MCP/agent-tooling developers:
1. Every agent host re-invents MCP registration + lifecycle hooks with incompatible config files, root keys, formats (JSON/JSONC/TOML/YAML/exported TS), transports, scopes, and event names.
2. No host reports per-tool token usage back to an MCP server — so "which of my tools cost the most context?" is otherwise unanswerable.

Declare once with `defineConnector({...})`; the CLI detects installed hosts, renders each native config, installs/syncs/uninstalls, and measures your server's own token footprint locally.

## Two pillars

- **Single-API multi-platform deployment.** One declarative + programmatic config → adapters render it into each platform's native MCP + hook + content config; one CLI installs/syncs/uninstalls everywhere.
- **Default per-MCP token telemetry.** Platform-independent, local-first, privacy-preserving (aggregate counts, never content). On by default, opt-out granular.

## Surfaces (declare once, deploy everywhere)

- `server` — one transport-polymorphic MCP server (stdio | http | sse | ws).
- `hooks` — normalized lifecycle hooks (SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, …).
- `commands` — slash commands (content files).
- `skills` — Agent Skills (SKILL.md folders + resources).
- `subagents` — named subagents (system prompt + tool/model scoping).

## Quick start

```bash
npm i -g agentconnect
cd my-mcp-project            # contains agentconnect.config.mjs
agentconnect detect       # which hosts are installed here?
agentconnect install --dry-run   # preview every change, everywhere
agentconnect install             # deploy across detected hosts
agentconnect doctor              # per-platform health checks
agentconnect telemetry report    # per-tool token footprint
```

## Key API: defineConnector fields

- `id` (required, kebab-case), `displayName`, `version`.
- `server?: ServerDef` — `transport`, `command`/`args`/`env`/`cwd` (stdio) or `url`/`headers`/`auth` (remote), `tools`, `timeoutMs`, `enabled`, `wrapForTelemetry`.
- `hooks?: HooksConfig` — per event: `{ matcher?, handler(evt) }`; handler returns `{ decision: allow|deny|modify|context|ask, reason?, updatedInput?, additionalContext?, updatedOutput? }` or void.
- `commands?`, `skills?`, `subagents?` — content-only surfaces.
- `telemetry?: TelemetryConfig` — `enabled` (default true), `modelFamilyHint`, `measureToolDefs`, `hostNativeUsage`, `store`, `calibration`.
- `platforms?` — per-platform overrides / escape hatch (`extra`, disable a surface, force scope).
- `targets?` — `"auto"` (default, all detected) or an explicit `PlatformId[]`.

Validation: throws `ConnectorConfigError` on bad id, non-function handler, missing required field, duplicate surface name, skill description >1024 chars, or unsafe `resources` path.

## CLI commands

- `detect` — list installed platforms, scope, capabilities, hook paradigm.
- `install [--scope user|project] [--targets a,b] [--connector path] [--dry-run]`
- `sync` — idempotent re-render; heals the stable home pointer.
- `uninstall [--connector-id <id>] [--targets …] [--dry-run]`
- `doctor [--targets …] [--json]` — health checks; non-zero exit on FAIL.
- `update [--channel stable|latest]` — managed update + pointer refresh (never silent).
- `telemetry report|export|leaderboard` — per-MCP token telemetry.
- `usage report|export|leaderboard` — host-native usage parsed read-only from CLI logs.
- `leaderboard` — unified: 🔌 per-MCP · 🖥️ host/user · 🛰️ live host-native turns (never summed).
- `hook`, `serve`, `usage-event` — internal entrypoints hosts point at.

## Supported platforms by paradigm (28)

- `json-stdio` (full hook dispatch): claude-code, codex, cursor, vscode-copilot, jetbrains-copilot, copilot-cli, gemini-cli, qwen-code, kiro, kimi, crush, goose, hermes, antigravity, antigravity-cli.
- `mcp-only` (MCP registration only): warp, kilo, droid, roo-code, trae, zed, amp, codebuff, mux, pi.
- `ts-plugin` (generated bridge module): opencode, omp, openclaw.

## Telemetry / leaderboards

- Default tokenizer: `gpt-tokenizer` (pure-JS); `o200k_base` for OpenAI/Codex, documented approximation for Anthropic; falls back to `chars/4` heuristic. Every row tagged `tokenizer-exact | tokenizer-approx | heuristic | host-native`.
- Two distinct leaderboards: per-MCP server bytes (telemetry) vs host CLI usage (usage). They measure different things and are never summed.
- Privacy: aggregate counts only; zero egress by default. Opt out: `AGENTCONNECT_TELEMETRY=0` or `telemetry: { enabled: false }`.
